text: Joseph went and told Pharaoh his father and brothers had arrived from Canaan with their flocks, herds, and belongings, and were now in Goshen.
He chose five of his brothers to present to Pharaoh.
Pharaoh asked their occupation; they said, your servants are shepherds, as were our fathers.
They said they had come to live there for a while, since the famine was severe in Canaan, and asked to settle in Goshen.
Pharaoh told Joseph his father and brothers had come to him.
The land of Egypt is before you; settle them in the best part, in Goshen; if capable men are among them, put them in charge of my livestock.
Joseph brought his father Jacob and presented him to Pharaoh, and Jacob blessed Pharaoh.
Pharaoh asked Jacob how old he was.
Jacob said, the years of my pilgrimage are 130; my years have been few and difficult, not as many as my fathers'.
Then Jacob blessed Pharaoh again and left his presence.
Joseph settled his father and brothers in Egypt, giving them property in the best part of the land, the district of Rameses, as Pharaoh directed.
Joseph provided food for his father, brothers, and all his father's household, according to family size.
There was no food in the whole region because the famine was severe; Egypt and Canaan wasted away.
Joseph collected all the money in Egypt and Canaan in payment for grain, and brought it to Pharaoh's palace.
When the money ran out, the Egyptians came asking for food, saying their money was all gone.
Joseph said, bring your livestock, and I will sell you food in exchange for it.
So they brought livestock to Joseph, and he gave them food in exchange for their horses, sheep, cattle, and donkeys that year.
The next year they came again and said, we cannot hide from our lord that our money and livestock are gone; nothing is left but our bodies and our land.
Why should we perish before your eyes? Buy us and our land in exchange for food, and we with our land will be in bondage to Pharaoh; give us seed so we may live and not die.
So Joseph bought all the land in Egypt for Pharaoh, since the Egyptians sold their fields because the famine was too severe for them, and the land became Pharaoh's.
Joseph reduced the people to servitude, from one end of Egypt to the other.
However, he did not buy the priests' land, because they had a regular allotment from Pharaoh and did not need to sell it.
Joseph said to the people, I have bought you and your land for Pharaoh today; here is seed for you to plant.
When the crop comes in, give a fifth of it to Pharaoh; the other four-fifths you may keep as seed and food.
They said, you have saved our lives; may we find favor and be Pharaoh's servants.
So Joseph established a law still in force today: a fifth of the produce belongs to Pharaoh, except priestly land.
Israel settled in Egypt, in Goshen; there they acquired property, were fruitful, and increased greatly.
Jacob lived in Egypt seventeen years, so his life span was 147 years.
As death approached, Jacob called Joseph and asked him to swear to bury him not in Egypt but with his fathers.
When I rest with my fathers, carry me out of Egypt and bury me where they are buried; Joseph said, I will do as you say.
Jacob said, swear it; Joseph swore, and Israel worshiped, leaning on the top of his staff.
